title: 天皇賞（春）の後に・敗戦の喜び

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
But that's the best I could've done... So I feel kind
of okay.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text: Nice work, McQueen. You beat me.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
You really are incredible. I'm disappointed
obviously, but I'm also kind of happy, hehe...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
Ryan, happy even after a loss... That carefree
expression on your face is a curious thing.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
But it's only natural that I beat you at this
distance. I've been working toward it my entire
life.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The victory belongs to Mejiro McQueen, but the
battle to get there was a fierce one.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
Next, I'll surpass you in your specialty, the
medium-distance race.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
A medium-distance race... Which one would that
be? Any ideas, Trainer?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The medium-distance championship in the spring
is the Takarazuka Kinen. In response to Mejiro
McQueen, we make that our goal.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
But in the end, it's the fans who will decide what
happens, leaving us unsure of how high we can
aim for.
